From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Defunct LGBT publication in Portland, Oregon, U.S.

Just Out

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| A *Just Out* cover story on [Storm Large](/wiki/Storm_Large "Storm Large") | |
| Type | Monthly magazine |
| Format | Glossy Magazine |
| Owner(s) | Glenn-Kipp Publishing, Inc |
| Publisher | Jonathan Kipp |
| Founded | 1983 |
| Ceased publication | 2013 |
| Language | English |
| Headquarters | [Portland, Oregon](/wiki/Portland,_Oregon "Portland, Oregon") |
| [Circulation](/wiki/Newspaper_circulation "Newspaper circulation") | [Portland metropolitan area](/wiki/Portland_metropolitan_area "Portland metropolitan area") |
| Website | [justout.com](http://www.justout.com) |

***Just Out*** was an [LGBTQ](/wiki/LGBTQ "LGBTQ") publication in [Portland](/wiki/Portland,_Oregon "Portland, Oregon"), [Oregon](/wiki/Oregon "Oregon") founded in 1983 by Jay Brown and Renee LaChance.[[1]](#cite_note-5years-1)[[2]](#cite_note-25years-2)
It ceased publication as a semimonthly newspaper in December 2011. In February 2012, Glenn-Kipp Publishing, Inc purchased the Just Out brand assets.[[3]](#cite_note-justoutreturns-3) *Just Out* ceased being published as a monthly LGBTQ magazine in February 2013.

The magazine was available for free at hundreds of businesses across the [Portland metropolitan area](/wiki/Portland_metropolitan_area "Portland metropolitan area"), and free digital copies are available as [PDF](/wiki/Portable_Document_Format "Portable Document Format") files on the web site.

Former contributors to *Just Out* include novelist [Marc Acito](/wiki/Marc_Acito "Marc Acito"), [Pink Martini](/wiki/Pink_Martini "Pink Martini") pianist [Thomas Lauderdale](/wiki/Thomas_Lauderdale "Thomas Lauderdale"), former [Willamette Week](/wiki/Willamette_Week "Willamette Week") [art director](/wiki/Art_director "Art director") and [Cathartic Comics](/w/index.php?title=Cathartic_Comics&action=edit&redlink=1 "Cathartic Comics (page does not exist)") founder [Rupert Kinnard](/wiki/Rupert_Kinnard "Rupert Kinnard"), and reporter and author [Peter Zuckerman](/wiki/Peter_Zuckerman "Peter Zuckerman").[[2]](#cite_note-25years-2) *[USA Today](/wiki/USA_Today "USA Today")* best-selling author [Andy Mangels](/wiki/Andy_Mangels "Andy Mangels") also wrote for the newspaper multiple times over a 25-year period, mostly in the early 2000s.[[4]](#cite_note-4)

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Just_Out&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

The newspaper's web site used to list many outdoor boxes and other places where *Just Out* was distributed at no charge. These drop sites could be found throughout the Portland metro area. Up until the mid-2000s there were drop sites elsewhere in Oregon. By the time the paper stopped publication in December 2011, some communities across Oregon had begun receiving *Just Out* again, including two distribution points in [Klamath Falls](/wiki/Klamath_Falls,_Oregon "Klamath Falls, Oregon").[[5]](#cite_note-5)

On January 21, 2009, *Just Out*'s editorial board issued a statement[[6]](#cite_note-samadams-6)
calling on recently inaugurated [Portland mayor](/wiki/List_of_mayors_of_Portland,_Oregon "List of mayors of Portland, Oregon") [Sam Adams](/wiki/Sam_Adams_(Oregon_politician) "Sam Adams (Oregon politician)") to resign, in light of his admission that he had covered up a sexual relationship with a state legislative intern in order to avoid disruptions to his mayoral campaign.

Mid-March 2009, three employees — news editor Jaymee Cuti, arts-and-culture editor Jim Radosta, and art director Blake Martinez — resigned because they had not been paid in full since late January, nor on time for six months. Publisher Marty Davis cited the [economic recession](/wiki/Late-2000s_recession "Late-2000s recession") and a resulting downturn in ad revenue as the cause of financial troubles.[[7]](#cite_note-opbmarch2009-7)[[8]](#cite_note-wweekmarch2009-8)

On December 26, 2011, publisher Marty Davis announced that *Just Out* was out of business effective immediately,[[9]](#cite_note-outofbusiness-9) with its December 9 issue being its last.

On February 14, 2012, it was announced that Glenn-Kipp Publishing, Inc., had acquired *Just Out*, with Jonathan Kipp as the new publisher, and that *Just Out* would resume publication.[[3]](#cite_note-justoutreturns-3) Kipp had contributed to *Just Out* previously, for two years.[[10]](#cite_note-kippinterview-10) It returned as a monthly magazine on June 1, 2012, and ceased publication in February 2013.[[11]](#cite_note-backjune2012-11)

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Just_Out&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: See also")]
